title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Minnesota
description: This race is for Minnesota’s open Class II U.S. Senate seat after Sen. Tina Smith announced she will not seek re-election. It is a high-profile statewide contest in a state that has leaned Democratic in recent federal elections, with the Democratic primary featuring Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan and Rep. Angie Craig and the Republican field including Adam Schwarze, Christopher Brooks, and Raymond Petersen. The main contrasts so far are elected-office experience versus outsider or military backgrounds, along with differences over economic policy, public safety, and national partisan direction.
polling_note: No candidate-specific public head-to-head polling for the Minnesota U.S. Senate race was confirmed from credible sources in the available search results; one broader Minnesota 2026 poll was found but did not provide a Senate matchup in the accessible snippet.

candidates.name: Peggy Flanagan
candidates.summary: Peggy Flanagan is Minnesota’s lieutenant governor and launched her U.S. Senate campaign in February 2026 after Sen. Tina Smith announced she would not seek re-election. Since entering the race, she has emphasized progressive priorities and has reported early endorsements and fundraising momentum.

summary_sources.title: Minnesota lieutenant governor launches Senate bid
Lt. Governor Peggy Flanagan Releases Key Numbers in U.S. Senate Campaign
Minnesota Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan announces run for U.S. Senate

career_history.title: State Representative
career_history.description: Represented a Minnesota House district before becoming lieutenant governor.

career_history.title: Organizer / advocacy leader
career_history.description: Worked in organizing and advocacy before elected office.

candidates.name: Adam Schwarze
candidates.summary: Adam Schwarze is a Republican candidate for Minnesota’s U.S. Senate seat and describes himself as a lifelong Minnesotan and veteran. His campaign website says he is running to bring common-sense government leadership and an outsider perspective to Washington.

summary_sources.title: Adam Schwarze: Minnesota Veteran for U.S. Senate
Conservative Leadership for a Stronger America - Adam Schwarze
Adam Schwarze announces candidacy for US Senate

career_history.title: Navy SEAL
career_history.description: Campaign biography describes him as a former Navy SEAL.

career_history.title: Marine infantry veteran
career_history.description: Campaign biography describes him as a former Marine infantry servicemember.

candidates.name: Christopher Brooks
candidates.summary: Christopher Brooks is a Republican candidate in Minnesota’s 2026 U.S. Senate race. His campaign website uses the slogan “service before politics, people before power” and presents him as an outsider candidate focused on reform themes.

summary_sources.title: Meet Chris - Brooks for U.S. Senate

candidates.name: Raymond Petersen
candidates.summary: Raymond Petersen is a Republican candidate for Minnesota’s U.S. Senate seat who describes himself as a common-sense conservative and independent Republican. His campaign materials emphasize term limits, anti-establishment themes, and opposition to what he characterizes as entrenched politics in Washington.

summary_sources.title: Raymond Petersen for US Senator from Minnesota
Position Outline — Raymond Petersen for US Senator from Minnesota

career_history.title: Candidate / community resident
career_history.description: Campaign biography says he is a lifelong Minnesota resident; no prior elected office was identified in the accessible sources.

candidates.name: Tina Smith
candidates.summary: Tina Smith is the incumbent U.S. senator from Minnesota and announced in February 2025 that she will not seek re-election in 2026. Her Senate biography says she has focused on health care, education, and family economic issues during her time in office.

summary_sources.title: U.S. Senator Tina Smith Pens Op-Ed Announcing She Will Not Seek Re-election in 2026
